days.label: Weekend
days.text: The Fourth itself spreads out across the region. In town, the Burlington Farmers Market sets up 70 plus vendors at 345 Pine Street from 9 to 2, and the Vermont Lake Monsters play an afternoon holiday game at Centennial Field at 1:05, with a Champ bobblehead for the first 1,000 fans. Coffee people can drift into our favorite regular Saturday activity, the BTown Coffee Club [MEETUP] at Zero Gravity from 10 to noon. Just outside the city, Colchester throws its 55th annual celebration with a morning parade, a 5K fun run, and fireworks at dusk, Richmond fills Volunteers Green all day and closes with fireworks at 9:15, and Essex Junction pairs the Revolution Run in the morning with Sticks and Stones and a big fireworks finish at the Champlain Valley Expo that night. For something more orchestral, the Vermont Symphony brings its America 250 summer tour to Shelburne at 7:30, fireworks included. Sunday cools things off nicely for the Winooski Farmers Market from 10 to 2, a Latin dance night cruise aboard the Spirit of Ethan Allen, another Lake Monsters game with a Jeep theme and duck giveaway, a Sunday polo match in Shelburne, and late night karaoke at Einstein Tap House. Running all week and well beyond, the Green Mountain Chamber Music Festival fills St. Michael's with young string players through the summer, the America 250 exhibit at Shelburne Museum runs through late October, and free community tours of the new Burlington High School are open for select times.
